summary: Merged regression coverage for QuantChat tunnel verification and advanced messaging UI work. Core auth/chat APIs passed, advanced messaging endpoints were verified, and the responsive chat shell now uses a single visible panel flow to avoid duplicate hidden-layout selector conflicts.

integration_issues.issue: Textarea interaction was previously flaky in automation-heavy flows, but the user-facing compose/send flow works in manual verification.

passed_tests: Frontend: chat selection and message display
Public tunnel: /api/health, /api/auth/me, user search, conversation create, send/get messages
action_items: Proceed with DNS cutover from Hostinger parking to Azure VM when networking (public 80/443) is opened.
Remove backend default FRONTEND_URL fallback and require env-only config for stricter production parity.
Split backend/server.py into smaller route/service modules for maintainability.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py remains oversized and should be split into smaller modules.
FRONTEND_URL currently has a localhost default; env-only URL config is preferred for production parity.
Auth cookies are still configured with secure=false and should be hardened for HTTPS production traffic.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tunnel URL tested: https://get-painting-consumers-completing.trycloudflare.com. Advanced messaging APIs are covered by backend tests, and the chat UI now renders a single active panel per breakpoint to reduce visibility ambiguity during automation.
rca_of_previous_issue: The earlier selector ambiguity came from rendering both mobile and desktop trees with duplicate data-testid values. The merged chat shell now swaps between a single visible left panel and chat area instead of keeping duplicate hidden layouts active.
